title: Book Trilogy Showcase

fields.vision: Book Trilogy Showcase is the reader-facing exhibit for a governed three-book manuscript system.
fields.mechanism: It presents the trilogy through public book order, canonical IDs, seventy-two chapter profiles, ReaderEdition targets, FinalProof argument backbone, ConstructionKits, PassageTargets, Democratic Dialectic voice material, book-specific registers, chapter shape, bridge logic, dependency logic, terminology controls, visual identity, and rewrite-state limits.
fields.built_components: Built components visible or source-backed in this package: a public book-order map, canonical IDs, seventy-two chapter profiles, chapter types, chapter claims, carry-forward logic, reader-load/citation metadata where exposed, source-bound passage signals, governed rewrite-state metadata, priority source traceability, and a no-image Porkbun package that spends its size budget on native substance instead of generic artwork.
fields.source_utilization: Arendt and political theory support amor mundi, action, judgment, plurality, and the public world; security and intelligence literature supports Book I's restraint, secrecy, coercion, and analytic-integrity claims; peace/conflict and trauma literature supports Book III's repair mechanisms; Democratic Dialectic .docx supplies authorial cadence and conceptual ore, not paste-ready final prose.
fields.plan: Execution plan: preserve canonical IDs; keep public order B2, B1, B3; upgrade each chapter by dossier rather than by freeform rewrite; classify every paragraph by rewrite mode; enforce book-specific register laws; run cross-book contamination checks; then replace public chapter prose only after evidence, citation, and connoisseurship review are complete.
fields.status: Status is deliberately conservative. The chapter map, governance scaffolds, source traces, and rewrite dossiers exist as public-representable evidence of the project architecture. The governed rewrite-state file explicitly says live chapter prose has not yet been replaced and public prose admission is not complete; this page should not claim final manuscript completion.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: final title review, per-chapter source normalization, quotation placement, final public chapter prose, accessibility review, and full connoisseurship review for whether the prose reaches the standard of the strongest Democratic Dialectic passages without letting Book II's voice contaminate Book I or Book III.
fields.detailed_source_vision: The trilogy is a governed three-book inquiry into power and the person: how dangerous power can be held without self-betrayal; how the individual capable of democratic speech is formed; and how violence, memory, and repair move across private, institutional, and political scales. The public page must therefore show not only that seventy-two chapters exist, but what each chapter is for.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The working mechanism is a manuscript workbench: ReaderEdition chapters provide the public rewrite target; FinalProof preserves argument backbone; ConstructionKits govern chapter shape; PassageTargets provide sentence pressure and source-bearing formulations; the Master Implementation Workbook governs bridge, dependency, and terminology logic; Democratic Dialectic .docx supplies a selective voice reservoir, especially for Public Book II.

source_derived_signals.text: Latest chapter-control addendum loaded for current-focus chapters; it updates chapter-level rebuild context, not the canonical prose source.

This chapter is in the latest refinement set. The editorial work is aimed at sharpening its pressure points while keeping the source machinery behind the prose.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: Book Trilogy Showcase is the reader-facing exhibit for a governed three-book manuscript system.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It presents the trilogy through public book order, canonical IDs, seventy-two chapter profiles, ReaderEdition targets, FinalProof argument backbone, ConstructionKits, PassageTargets, Democratic Dialectic voice material, book-specific registers, chapter shape, bridge logic, dependency logic, terminology controls, visual identity, and rewrite-state limits.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because a reader should see what the books are arguing and how the chapter system is governed, while also seeing that final public prose admission remains unfinished rather than completed.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
